source.title: Structural Damage - Injury During Slip and Cut Drill Line Operations

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Keathley Canyon Block 785

event.incident_type: Personnel Injury - Slip/Trip/Fall with Struck By
actions_taken: Employee reported to Rig Medic with sore shoulder
Given over-the-counter medication and ice pack
Employee evacuated to shore on 8 March 2024 by regularly scheduled helicopter
Medical evaluation diagnosed small tear (torn labrum) and strain to back of left shoulder
Physical therapy prescribed for 10 to 12 weeks
event.summary: On 7 March 2024 at 1500 hours, a Seadrill employee sustained a back and shoulder injury during slip and cut drill line operations on the Seadrill West Neptune drillship. While attempting to correct an abnormal twist in a 2-inch diameter drill line using an air tugger and nylon web sling, the drill line slipped from the sling and fell approximately 20-25 feet. The 19-foot section weighing 148 pounds struck the employee who had tripped while exiting the line of fire, hitting his hardhat, rolling over his middle back and left shoulder. He was diagnosed with a torn labrum and required 10-12 weeks of physical therapy.
key_phrases: slip and cut drill line operations
nylon web sling lost its bite

threats.name: Improper rigging of drill line with nylon web sling
Abnormal twist in drill line caused by tying off to cat head
Deviation from standard slip and cut procedures
Personnel positioned in drop zone during lifting operations

consequences.name: Personnel injury - torn labrum and shoulder strain
Lost time injury requiring evacuation and extended recovery
Regulatory non-compliance - failure to operate in safe and workmanlike manner

controls.name: Permit to Work Standard Operating Manual for Slip & Cut Drill Line
controls.barrier_role: Procedural guidance for drill line operations
controls.lod_basis: Primary procedural control for task execution

supporting_text: the Permit to Work SOM for slip and cut drill line lacked instructions
Job procedures not followed. Poor pre-job task and the Permit to Work Standard Operation Manual for slip and cut drill line lacked instructions

controls.name: Pre-job task planning and risk assessment
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and plan safe work execution
controls.lod_basis: Primary planning control before task execution

supporting_text: Poor pre-job task and the Permit to Work Standard Operation Manual for slip and cut drill line lacked instructions
The Seadrill Crew decided to utilize an air tugger and nylon web sling to lift and uncoil the abnormal twist

controls.name: Supervision of drill line operations
controls.barrier_role: Oversight and verification of safe work practices

supporting_text: Inadequate supervision. The Supervisor for the slip and cut drill line operations was not aware of the Seadrill Crew's plan of using a nylon web sling
lack of supervision

controls.name: Communication between Derrickman and Driller
controls.barrier_role: Ensure shared understanding of work plan and hazards

supporting_text: The Derrickman informed the Driller that the crew will be lifting the drill line, but he failed to communicate how the drill line was going to be lifted

Inadequate job instructions provided. The Driller was informed by the Derrickman about that the Seadrill Crew's decision to lift the 2-inch drill line, but he failed to communicate to the Driller how this operation was going to be performed
poor communication
Inadequate management of change procedures. The Seadrill Crew deviated from the normal procedures for slip and cut drill line by tying of the drill line to the Cat Head
the crew deviated from the normal procedures for slip and cut drill line

controls.name: Proper rigging equipment selection and use
controls.barrier_role: Ensure adequate load securement during lifting
controls.lod_basis: Primary physical control for load handling

supporting_text: Improper Use of Equipment. The improper use a nylon web sling that lost its bite allowed the 2-inch diameter drill line to slip through the sling
the nylon web sling lost its bite that allowed 19 feet of the 2-inch diameter drill line weighing 148 pounds to fall freely to the rig floor
A nylon web sling was then placed about 4 feet from the end of the drill line

controls.name: Drop zone exclusion and line of fire management
controls.barrier_role: Keep personnel clear of suspended load hazards
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent personnel exposure

supporting_text: A Seadrill employee, situated in the line of fire attempted to move out of the way
As he was exiting the area, he tripped over the coiled drill line

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment - Hard Hat
controls.barrier_role: Reduce severity of head injury from falling objects
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to protect personnel

supporting_text: The falling section of the drill line, about 19 feet in length and 148 pounds in weight, struck the rear of his hardhat

controls.name: Onsite medical response and Rig Medic
controls.barrier_role: Provide immediate medical care to injured personnel
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to minimize injury severity

supporting_text: The employee reported to the Rig Medic with a sore shoulder and was given over-the-counter medication and an ice pack
An onshore physician was consulted

controls.name: Medical evacuation capability
controls.barrier_role: Transport injured personnel to advanced medical care

supporting_text: it was decided to transport the employee to shore for a further medical evaluation
On 8 March 2024, the Seadrill employee was transported from the drillship to shore by a regularly scheduled helicopter

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
